# UC-22.14.68 · Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity — TLS Policy Downgrades

## Description

Detects servers offering weak TLS versions or cipher suites for SC-8 transmission confidentiality and integrity.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes net and sourcetypes stream:tls. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Enrich with asset owner; (2) emergency exceptions with expiry; (3) track remediation in RA-7; (4) validate after patch window. Indexes required: `index=net`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=stream:tls`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809) or certificate inventory.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `stream:tls` or `tenable:` plugin SSL findings.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enrich with asset owner; (2) emergency exceptions with expiry; (3) track remediation in RA-7; (4) validate after patch window.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=net sourcetype=stream:tls earliest=-7d version IN ("SSLv3","TLS1.0","TLS1.1")
| stats dc(src_ip) as clients, values(cipher) as ciphers by dest_ip, dest_port
| sort - clients
```

Understanding this SPL

**Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity — TLS Policy Downgrades (SC-8)** — Detects servers offering weak TLS versions or cipher suites for SC-8 transmission confidentiality and integrity.

Documented **Data sources**: `stream:tls` or `tenable:` plugin SSL findings. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809) or certificate inventory. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: net; **sourcetype**: stream:tls. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=net, sourcetype=stream:tls, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by dest_ip, dest_port** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (weak TLS services), Bar chart (by cipher), Single value (affected IPs)

## SPL

```spl
index=net sourcetype=stream:tls earliest=-7d version IN ("SSLv3","TLS1.0","TLS1.1")
| stats dc(src_ip) as clients, values(cipher) as ciphers by dest_ip, dest_port
| sort - clients
```

## Visualization

Table (weak TLS services), Bar chart (by cipher), Single value (affected IPs). Key fields: `ciphers`, `clients`, `dest_ip`, `dest_port`.
